Name: sort
Description: Parent category for various sorting algorithms in C++.

Name: Bubble Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

Name: Selection Sort
Description: An in-place comparison sorting algorithm that divides the input list into a sorted and an unsorted sublist.

Name: Insertion Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that builds the final sorted array one item at a time.

Name: Merge Sort
Description: An efficient, stable, comparison-based sorting algorithm based on the divide and conquer paradigm.

Name: Quick Sort
Description: An efficient sorting algorithm that works by selecting a 'pivot' element and partitioning the other elements into two sub-arrays.

Name: Heap Sort
Description: A comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure.

Name: Counting Sort
Description: An integer sorting algorithm that operates by counting the number of objects that have each distinct key value.

Name: Radix Sort
Description: A non-comparative sorting algorithm that sorts integers by processing individual digits.

Name: Bucket Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm that works by distributing the elements of an array into a number of buckets.

Name: Shell Sort
Description: A highly efficient, in-place, comparison-based sorting algorithm which is a generalization of insertion sort.

Name: Comb Sort
Description: Improves on Bubble Sort by using a gap of size more than 1, helping to eliminate 'turtles' quickly.

Name: Pancake Sort
Description: The only allowed operation is to flip a prefix of the array. The goal is to sort the array with the minimum number of flips.

Name: Gnome Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm similar to insertion sort but moving the element to its proper place is accomplished by a series of swaps.

Name: Cocktail Shaker Sort
Description: A variation of Bubble Sort that sorts in both directions on each pass through the list.

Name: STL Sort (IntroSort)
Description: The C++ STL std::sort function. It is a highly optimized, hybrid sorting algorithm, typically IntroSort (a combination of QuickSort, HeapSort, and InsertionSort).

Name: data_structure
Description: Parent category for various data structures in C++.

Name: Singly Linked List
Description: A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes, and each node points to the next node in the sequence.

Name: Stack
Description: An STL container adapter that provides a LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) data structure.

Name: Queue
Description: An STL container adapter that provides a FIFO (First-In, First-Out) data structure.

Name: Binary Search Tree
Description: A node-based binary tree data structure with the property that the left subtree of a node contains only nodes with keys lesser than the node’s key.

Name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
Description: A tree-like data structure that proves to be quite efficient for solving problems related to strings and prefixes.

Name: Min-Heap (Priority Queue)
Description: A specialized tree-based data structure that satisfies the heap property. In C++, it's easily implemented using std::priority_queue with a custom comparator.

Name: Hash Table (unordered_map)
Description: A data structure that maps keys to values. std::unordered_map is the C++ STL implementation of a hash table.

Name: Doubly Linked List
Description: A linked list where each node has a pointer to the next node and a pointer to the previous node.

Name: Graph (Adjacency List)
Description: A data structure representing a graph as a collection of adjacency lists, one for each vertex in the graph.

Name: Disjoint Set Union (DSU)
Description: A data structure that stores a collection of disjoint sets, commonly used for cycle detection in graphs (Kruskal's algorithm).

Name: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
Description: A data structure that can efficiently update elements and calculate prefix sums in a table of numbers.

Name: Segment Tree
Description: A tree data structure for storing information about intervals, or segments. It allows querying which of the stored segments contain a given point.

Name: Priority Queue (Max-Heap)
Description: The default std::priority_queue in C++ is a Max-Heap, where the largest element is always at the top.

Name: Deque
Description: A double-ended queue provided by the C++ STL, allowing fast insertions and deletions at both its beginning and its end.

Name: STL Set (Red-Black Tree)
Description: A container that stores unique elements in a sorted order. It is typically implemented as a self-balancing binary search tree (Red-Black Tree).

Name: search
Description: Parent category for various search algorithms in C++.

Name: Linear Search
Description: A method for finding an element within a list. It sequentially checks each element of the list until a match is found or the whole list has been searched.

Name: Binary Search
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding an item from a sorted list of items by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.

Name: Recursive Binary Search
Description: A recursive implementation of the binary search algorithm.

Name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. The algorithm starts at the root node and explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

Name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. It explores the neighbor nodes at the present depth prior to moving on to nodes at the next depth level.

Name: STL lower_bound
Description: Returns an iterator pointing to the first element in the range [first, last) which does not compare less than val. This is an efficient way to find the first occurrence of an element in a sorted range.

Name: STL upper_bound
Description: Returns an iterator pointing to the first element in the range [first, last) which compares greater than val. Useful for counting occurrences or finding the insertion point for new elements.

Name: Jump Search
Description: A searching algorithm for sorted arrays. The basic idea is to check fewer elements by jumping ahead by fixed steps.

Name: Interpolation Search
Description: An improvement over Binary Search for instances where the values in a sorted array are uniformly distributed.

Name: Exponential Search
Description: An algorithm for searching sorted, unbounded/infinite lists. It can also be used to search in bounded arrays by first finding a range and then performing a binary search.

Name: Ternary Search
Description: A decrease and conquer search algorithm that splits the array into three parts and determines which part the key lies in.

Name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Finds the minimum element in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Searches for a target value in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Count Occurrences using STL
Description: Counts the number of occurrences of a target value in a sorted array using std::lower_bound and std::upper_bound for O(log n) performance.

Name: Find Peak Element
Description: A peak element is an element that is strictly greater than its neighbors. This finds any peak element in O(log n) time.

Name: math
Description: Parent category for various mathematical algorithms in C++.

Name: Factorial
Description: Calculates the factorial of a non-negative integer.

Name: Fibonacci Sequence
Description: Generates the Fibonacci sequence up to the nth term.

Name: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
Description: Finds the largest positive integer that divides two integers without leaving a remainder using the Euclidean algorithm.

Name: Check for Prime Number
Description: Checks if a given integer is a prime number.

Name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding all prime numbers up to a specified integer.

Name: Modular Exponentiation
Description: Calculates (base^exp) % mod efficiently, also known as power in modular arithmetic.

Name: Combination (nCr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose r items from a set of n items without regard to order.

Name: Permutation (nPr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose and arrange r items from a set of n items.

Name: Palindrome Number Check
Description: Checks if a number is a palindrome without converting it to a string.

Name: Matrix Multiplication
Description: Performs matrix multiplication on two given matrices.

Name: Catalan Numbers
Description: Calculates the nth Catalan number using dynamic programming.

Name: Euler's Totient Function
Description: Counts the positive integers up to a given integer n that are relatively prime to n.

Name: Extended Euclidean Algorithm
Description: Computes the coefficients of Bézout's identity, which are integers x and y such that ax + by = gcd(a, b).

Name: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
Description: Calculates the smallest positive integer that is divisible by both a and b.

Name: Integer Square Root
Description: Calculates the floor of the square root of an integer using binary search.

Name: dynamic_programming
Description: Parent category for dynamic programming algorithms in C++.

Name: Fibonacci (Memoization)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a top-down dynamic programming approach (memoization).

Name: Fibonacci (Tabulation)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a bottom-up dynamic programming approach (tabulation).

Name: 0/1 Knapsack Problem
Description: Given weights and values of n items, put these items in a knapsack of capacity W to get the maximum total value.

Name: Longest Common Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence present in two given sequences.

Name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence of a given sequence in which the subsequence's elements are in sorted order.

Name: Edit Distance
Description: Finds the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions or substitutions) required to change one word into the other.

Name: Coin Change Problem
Description: Finds the minimum number of coins that you need to make up a given amount.

Name: Subset Sum Problem
Description: Determines if a given set can be partitioned into two subsets such that the sum of elements in both subsets is the same.

Name: Rod Cutting Problem
Description: Given a rod of length n and prices for pieces, determine the maximum value obtainable by cutting up the rod and selling the pieces.

Name: Matrix Chain Multiplication
Description: Determines the optimal parenthesization of a chain of matrix multiplications to minimize the number of scalar multiplications.

Name: Word Break Problem
Description: Determine if a string can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.

Name: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's)
Description: Finds the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers which has the largest sum.

Name: Binomial Coefficient
Description: Calculates the binomial coefficient C(n, k) using dynamic programming.

Name: Unbounded Knapsack
Description: A variation of the knapsack problem where we can use one or more instances of any item.

Name: House Robber
Description: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. You cannot rob adjacent houses. Find the maximum amount of money you can rob.

Name: strings
Description: Parent category for string manipulation algorithms in C++.

Name: Check for Palindrome
Description: Checks if a given string is a palindrome.

Name: Reverse a String
Description: Reverses the characters of a given string in-place.

Name: Check for Anagrams
Description: Checks if two strings are anagrams of each other.

Name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Description: Finds the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Name: Valid Parentheses
Description: Determines if a string of parentheses is valid.

Name: Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) Search
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding occurrences of a 'word' W within a main text string T.

Name: Rabin-Karp Algorithm
Description: A string-searching algorithm that uses hashing to find any one of a set of pattern strings in a text.

Name: Group Anagrams
Description: Groups a list of strings into sublists of anagrams.

Name: Implement strStr()
Description: Finds the first occurrence of a substring (needle) in a string (haystack) using the built-in string::find method.

Name: Remove Duplicate Characters
Description: Removes duplicate characters from a string while preserving order.

Name: String to Integer (atoi)
Description: Converts a string to an integer, handling whitespace, signs, and overflow.

Name: Z-Algorithm
Description: A linear time string searching algorithm that preprocesses the string and creates a Z-array storing the length of the longest substring starting from str[i] which is also a prefix.

Name: Manacher's Algorithm
Description: An algorithm to find the longest palindromic substring in any given string in linear time.

Name: Longest Common Prefix
Description: Finds the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings.

Name: Reverse Words in a String
Description: Reverses the order of words in a given string.

Name: backtracking
Description: Parent category for backtracking algorithms in C++.

Name: Generate All Permutations
Description: Generates all possible permutations of a given list of numbers.

Name: Generate All Subsets (Power Set)
Description: Generates all possible subsets (the power set) of a given list of numbers.

Name: N-Queens Problem
Description: The N-Queens puzzle is the problem of placing N chess queens on an N×N chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other.

Name: Sudoku Solver
Description: Solves a Sudoku puzzle using a backtracking algorithm.

Name: Combination Sum
Description: Finds all unique combinations in a list of candidate numbers where the numbers sum to a target.

Name: Word Search
Description: Given a 2D board and a word, find if the word exists in the grid.

Name: Rat in a Maze
Description: A rat starts from source (0,0) and has to reach the destination (N-1, N-1). The rat can move only forward and down.

Name: Generate Parentheses
Description: Generates all combinations of well-formed parentheses for a given number of pairs.

Name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
Description: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent.

Name: Palindrome Partitioning
Description: Given a string, partition it such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome.

Name: Permutations II (with duplicates)
Description: Given a collection of numbers that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations.

Name: Knight's Tour
Description: A sequence of moves of a knight on a chessboard such that the knight visits every square exactly once.

Name: M-Coloring Problem
Description: A problem of coloring the vertices of a graph with at most m colors such that no two adjacent vertices have the same color.

Name: Beautiful Arrangement
Description: Find the number of beautiful arrangements of numbers from 1 to n.

Name: Restore IP Addresses
Description: Given a string containing only digits, restore it by returning all possible valid IP address combinations.

Name: bit_manipulation
Description: Parent category for bit manipulation algorithms in C++.

Name: Check if a number is even or odd
Description: Checks if a number is even or odd using the bitwise AND operator.

Name: Count set bits (Brian Kernighan)
Description: Counts the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of an integer.

Name: Check if the i-th bit is set
Description: Checks if the bit at the i-th position is set to 1.

Name: Set the i-th bit
Description: Sets the bit at the i-th position to 1.

Name: Clear the i-th bit
Description: Clears the bit at the i-th position (sets it to 0).

Name: Toggle the i-th bit
Description: Toggles the bit at the i-th position (flips 0 to 1 and 1 to 0).

Name: Check if a number is a power of 2
Description: Checks if a given positive integer is a power of two.

Name: Find the single number in an array
Description: Given a non-empty array of integers where every element appears twice except for one, find that single one.

Name: Swap two numbers without a temporary variable
Description: Swaps the values of two integer variables using XOR operations.

Name: Find the two non-repeating elements
Description: Finds the two elements that appear only once in an array where all other elements appear twice.

Name: Gray Code Conversion
Description: Converts a binary number to its Gray code equivalent.

Name: Reverse bits of a given integer
Description: Reverses the bits of a 32-bit unsigned integer.

Name: Sum of two integers without using + and -
Description: Calculates the sum of two integers using bitwise operations.

Name: Find position of the only set bit
Description: If a number has only one set bit, this function returns the 1-based position of that bit.

Name: Number of Flips to Convert A to B
Description: Counts the number of bits you would need to flip to get from one integer to another by counting set bits in their XOR.

Name: other
Description: Parent category for miscellaneous but important algorithms in C++.

Name: Two Sum Problem
Description: Given an array of integers, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to a specific target.

Name: Kadane's Algorithm
Description: An efficient algorithm to find the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers that has the largest sum.

Name: Sliding Window Maximum
Description: Given an array and an integer k, find the maximum for each and every contiguous subarray of size k.

Name: LRU Cache
Description: A Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache organizes items in order of use, allowing you to quickly identify which item hasn't been used for the longest amount of time.

Name: Meeting Rooms II
Description: Given an array of meeting time intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

Name: Next Permutation
Description: Implement the next lexicographical permutation of a list of numbers.

Name: Dutch National Flag Problem
Description: Given an array containing 0s, 1s, and 2s (representing colors), sort them in-place.

Name: Josephus Problem
Description: In a circle of n people, every k-th person is eliminated. The problem is to find the position (0-indexed) of the last person remaining.

Name: Reservoir Sampling
Description: A randomized algorithm for choosing a simple random sample of k items from a population of unknown size n in a single pass.

Name: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm
Description: Finds the majority element in an array (the element that appears more than n/2 times) in linear time and constant space.

Name: Topological Sort
Description: A linear ordering of the vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge from vertex u to vertex v, u comes before v.

Name: Cycle Detection in a Linked List
Description: Detects if a linked list has a cycle using Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm.

Name: Find the Celebrity
Description: In a party of n people, a celebrity is a person who is known by everyone but knows no one. This finds the celebrity in O(n) time.

Name: Flood Fill Algorithm (BFS)
Description: An algorithm that determines the area connected to a given node in a multi-dimensional array.

Name: Trapping Rain Water
Description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

Name: sort
Description: Parent category for various sorting algorithms in C#.

Name: Bubble Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

Name: Selection Sort
Description: An in-place comparison sorting algorithm that divides the input list into a sorted and an unsorted sublist.

Name: Insertion Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that builds the final sorted array one item at a time.

Name: Merge Sort
Description: An efficient, stable, comparison-based sorting algorithm based on the divide and conquer paradigm.

Name: Quick Sort
Description: An efficient sorting algorithm that works by selecting a 'pivot' element and partitioning the other elements into two sub-arrays.

Name: Heap Sort
Description: A comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure.

Name: Counting Sort
Description: An integer sorting algorithm that operates by counting the number of objects that have each distinct key value.

Name: Radix Sort
Description: A non-comparative sorting algorithm that sorts integers by processing individual digits.

Name: Bucket Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm that works by distributing the elements of an array into a number of buckets.

Name: Shell Sort
Description: A highly efficient, in-place, comparison-based sorting algorithm which is a generalization of insertion sort.

Name: Comb Sort
Description: Improves on Bubble Sort by using a gap of size more than 1, helping to eliminate 'turtles' quickly.

Name: Pancake Sort
Description: The only allowed operation is to flip a prefix of the array. The goal is to sort the array with the minimum number of flips.

Name: Gnome Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm similar to insertion sort but moving the element to its proper place is accomplished by a series of swaps.

Name: Cocktail Shaker Sort
Description: A variation of Bubble Sort that sorts in both directions on each pass through the list.

Name: Array.Sort (IntroSort)
Description: The built-in sort method in C#. It is a highly optimized, unstable, hybrid sorting algorithm, typically IntroSort (a combination of QuickSort, HeapSort, and InsertionSort).

Name: data_structure
Description: Parent category for various data structures in C#.

Name: Singly Linked List
Description: A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes, and each node points to the next node in the sequence.

Name: Stack
Description: A generic collection that represents a last-in, first-out (LIFO) collection of objects.

Name: Queue
Description: A generic collection that represents a first-in, first-out (FIFO) collection of objects.

Name: Binary Search Tree
Description: A node-based binary tree data structure with the property that the left subtree of a node contains only nodes with keys lesser than the node’s key.

Name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
Description: A tree-like data structure that proves to be quite efficient for solving problems related to strings and prefixes.

Name: Min-Heap (PriorityQueue)
Description: A specialized data structure that satisfies the heap property. In C# (.NET 6+), PriorityQueue<TElement, TPriority> provides this functionality.

Name: Hash Table (Dictionary)
Description: A data structure that maps keys to values. The Dictionary<TKey, TValue> class is the C# generic implementation of a hash table.

Name: Doubly Linked List (LinkedList<T>)
Description: The built-in LinkedList<T> in C# is a generic doubly linked list, allowing insertion and deletion from both ends.

Name: Graph (Adjacency List)
Description: A data structure representing a graph as an array of adjacency lists.

Name: Disjoint Set Union (DSU)
Description: A data structure that stores a collection of disjoint sets, commonly used for cycle detection in graphs.

Name: HashSet
Description: Represents a set of values, providing high-performance set operations. It does not store duplicate elements.

Name: SortedList
Description: Represents a collection of key/value pairs that are sorted on the key. It combines features of a hash table and a sorted array.

Name: Max-Heap (Custom PriorityQueue)
Description: A Max-Heap can be implemented in .NET 6+ PriorityQueue by providing a reverse comparer.

Name: ObservableCollection
Description: Represents a dynamic data collection that provides notifications when items get added, removed, or when the whole list is refreshed.

Name: ConcurrentDictionary
Description: Represents a thread-safe collection of key/value pairs that can be accessed by multiple threads concurrently.

Name: search
Description: Parent category for various search algorithms in C#.

Name: Linear Search
Description: A method for finding an element within a list. It sequentially checks each element of the list until a match is found or the whole list has been searched.

Name: Binary Search (Iterative)
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding an item from a sorted list of items by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.

Name: Binary Search (Recursive)
Description: A recursive implementation of the binary search algorithm.

Name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. The algorithm starts at the root node and explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

Name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. It explores the neighbor nodes at the present depth prior to moving on to nodes at the next depth level.

Name: Array.BinarySearch
Description: Uses the built-in static method from the Array class to perform a binary search on a sorted 1D array.

Name: LINQ FirstOrDefault
Description: Finds the first element in a sequence that satisfies a condition. Returns the default value for the type if no such element is found.

Name: Jump Search
Description: A searching algorithm for sorted arrays. The basic idea is to check fewer elements by jumping ahead by fixed steps.

Name: Interpolation Search
Description: An improvement over Binary Search for instances where the values in a sorted array are uniformly distributed.

Name: Exponential Search
Description: An algorithm for searching sorted lists. It works by first finding a range where the element might be and then performing a binary search.

Name: Ternary Search
Description: A decrease and conquer search algorithm that splits the array into three parts and determines which part the key lies in.

Name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Finds the minimum element in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Searches for a target value in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Find First and Last Position of Element
Description: Finds the starting and ending position of a given target value in a sorted array.

Name: Find Peak Element
Description: A peak element is an element that is strictly greater than its neighbors. This finds any peak element in O(log n) time.

Name: math
Description: Parent category for various mathematical algorithms in C#.

Name: Factorial
Description: Calculates the factorial of a non-negative integer.

Name: Fibonacci Sequence
Description: Generates the Fibonacci sequence up to the nth term.

Name: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
Description: Finds the largest positive integer that divides two integers without leaving a remainder using the Euclidean algorithm.

Name: Check for Prime Number
Description: Checks if a given integer is a prime number.

Name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding all prime numbers up to a specified integer.

Name: Modular Exponentiation
Description: Calculates (base^exp) % mod efficiently, also known as power in modular arithmetic.

Name: Combination (nCr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose r items from a set of n items without regard to order.

Name: Permutation (nPr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose and arrange r items from a set of n items.

Name: Palindrome Number Check
Description: Checks if a number is a palindrome without converting it to a string.

Name: Matrix Multiplication
Description: Performs matrix multiplication on two given matrices.

Name: Catalan Numbers
Description: Calculates the nth Catalan number using the binomial coefficient formula.

Name: Euler's Totient Function
Description: Counts the positive integers up to a given integer n that are relatively prime to n.

Name: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
Description: Calculates the smallest positive integer that is divisible by both a and b.

Name: Integer Square Root
Description: Calculates the floor of the square root of an integer using binary search to prevent overflow.

Name: Happy Number
Description: A happy number is a number defined by the process: starting with any positive integer, replace the number by the sum of the squares of its digits, and repeat the process until the number equals 1, or it loops endlessly in a cycle which does not include 1.

Name: dynamic_programming
Description: Parent category for dynamic programming algorithms in C#.

Name: Fibonacci (Memoization)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a top-down dynamic programming approach (memoization).

Name: Fibonacci (Tabulation)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a bottom-up dynamic programming approach (tabulation).

Name: 0/1 Knapsack Problem
Description: Given weights and values of n items, put these items in a knapsack of capacity W to get the maximum total value.

Name: Longest Common Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence present in two given sequences.

Name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence of a given sequence in which the subsequence's elements are in sorted order.

Name: Edit Distance
Description: Finds the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions or substitutions) required to change one word into the other.

Name: Coin Change Problem
Description: Finds the minimum number of coins that you need to make up a given amount.

Name: Subset Sum Problem
Description: Determines if a given set can be partitioned into two subsets such that the sum of elements in both subsets is the same.

Name: Rod Cutting Problem
Description: Given a rod of length n and prices for pieces, determine the maximum value obtainable by cutting up the rod and selling the pieces.

Name: Word Break Problem
Description: Determine if a string can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.

Name: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's)
Description: Finds the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers which has the largest sum.

Name: Binomial Coefficient
Description: Calculates the binomial coefficient C(n, k) using dynamic programming with space optimization.

Name: Unbounded Knapsack
Description: A variation of the knapsack problem where we can use one or more instances of any item.

Name: House Robber
Description: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. You cannot rob adjacent houses. Find the maximum amount of money you can rob.

Name: Climbing Stairs
Description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

Name: strings
Description: Parent category for string manipulation algorithms in C#.

Name: Check for Palindrome
Description: Checks if a given string is a palindrome, ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.

Name: Reverse a String
Description: Reverses the characters of a given string.

Name: Check for Anagrams
Description: Checks if two strings are anagrams of each other (contain the same characters in different orders).

Name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Description: Finds the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Name: Valid Parentheses
Description: Determines if a string of parentheses is valid (e.g., '()[]{}' is valid).

Name: String Contains Substring
Description: Finds the first occurrence of a substring (needle) in a string (haystack) using the built-in IndexOf method.

Name: Group Anagrams
Description: Groups a list of strings into sublists of anagrams using LINQ.

Name: Remove Duplicate Characters
Description: Removes duplicate characters from a string. Note that this does not preserve the original order.

Name: Count Vowels and Consonants
Description: Counts the number of vowels and consonants in a given string using LINQ.

Name: String to Integer (atoi)
Description: Converts a string to an integer, handling whitespace, signs, and overflow.

Name: Longest Common Prefix
Description: Finds the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings.

Name: Reverse Words in a String
Description: Reverses the order of words in a given string.

Name: Manacher's Algorithm
Description: An algorithm to find the longest palindromic substring in any given string in linear time.

Name: Rabin-Karp Algorithm
Description: A string-searching algorithm that uses hashing to find any one of a set of pattern strings in a text.

Name: Caesar Cipher
Description: A simple substitution cipher where each letter in the plaintext is shifted a certain number of places down or up the alphabet.

Name: backtracking
Description: Parent category for backtracking algorithms in C#.

Name: Generate All Permutations
Description: Generates all possible permutations of a given list of numbers.

Name: Generate All Subsets (Power Set)
Description: Generates all possible subsets (the power set) of a given list of numbers.

Name: N-Queens Problem
Description: The N-Queens puzzle is the problem of placing N chess queens on an N×N chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other.

Name: Sudoku Solver
Description: Solves a Sudoku puzzle using a backtracking algorithm.

Name: Combination Sum
Description: Finds all unique combinations in a list of candidate numbers where the numbers sum to a target.

Name: Word Search
Description: Given a 2D board and a word, find if the word exists in the grid.

Name: Generate Parentheses
Description: Generates all combinations of well-formed parentheses for a given number of pairs.

Name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
Description: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent.

Name: Palindrome Partitioning
Description: Given a string, partition it such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome.

Name: Permutations II (with duplicates)
Description: Given a collection of numbers that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations.

Name: Beautiful Arrangement
Description: Find the number of beautiful arrangements of numbers from 1 to n.

Name: Restore IP Addresses
Description: Given a string containing only digits, restore it by returning all possible valid IP address combinations.

Name: Rat in a Maze
Description: A rat starts from source (0,0) and has to reach the destination (N-1, N-1).

Name: M-Coloring Problem
Description: A problem of coloring the vertices of a graph with at most m colors such that no two adjacent vertices have the same color.

Name: Path with Maximum Gold
Description: In a gold mine grid, find the path that collects the most gold.

Name: bit_manipulation
Description: Parent category for bit manipulation algorithms in C#.

Name: Check if a number is even or odd
Description: Checks if a number is even or odd using the bitwise AND operator.

Name: Count set bits (Brian Kernighan)
Description: Counts the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of an integer.

Name: Check if the i-th bit is set
Description: Checks if the bit at the i-th position is set to 1.

Name: Set the i-th bit
Description: Sets the bit at the i-th position to 1.

Name: Clear the i-th bit
Description: Clears the bit at the i-th position (sets it to 0).

Name: Toggle the i-th bit
Description: Toggles the bit at the i-th position (flips 0 to 1 and 1 to 0).

Name: Check if a number is a power of 2
Description: Checks if a given positive integer is a power of two.

Name: Find the single number in an array
Description: Given a non-empty array of integers where every element appears twice except for one, find that single one.

Name: Swap two numbers without a temporary variable
Description: Swaps the values of two integer variables using XOR operations.

Name: Find the two non-repeating elements
Description: Finds the two elements that appear only once in an array where all other elements appear twice.

Name: Gray Code Conversion
Description: Converts a binary number to its Gray code equivalent.

Name: Reverse bits of a given integer
Description: Reverses the bits of a 32-bit unsigned integer.

Name: Sum of two integers without using + and -
Description: Calculates the sum of two integers using bitwise operations.

Name: Find position of the only set bit
Description: If a number has only one set bit, this function returns the 1-based position of that bit.

Name: Number of Flips to Convert A to B
Description: Counts the number of bits you would need to flip to get from one integer to another by counting set bits in their XOR.

Name: other
Description: Parent category for miscellaneous but important algorithms in C#.

Name: Two Sum Problem
Description: Given an array of integers, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to a specific target.

Name: Kadane's Algorithm
Description: An efficient algorithm to find the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers that has the largest sum.

Name: Sliding Window Maximum
Description: Given an array and an integer k, find the maximum for each and every contiguous subarray of size k.

Name: LRU Cache
Description: A Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache organizes items in order of use, allowing you to quickly identify which item hasn't been used for the longest amount of time.

Name: Meeting Rooms II
Description: Given an array of meeting time intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

Name: Next Permutation
Description: Implement the next lexicographical permutation of a list of numbers.

Name: Dutch National Flag Problem
Description: Given an array containing 0s, 1s, and 2s (representing colors), sort them in-place.

Name: Josephus Problem
Description: In a circle of n people, every k-th person is eliminated. The problem is to find the position (0-indexed) of the last person remaining.

Name: Reservoir Sampling
Description: A randomized algorithm for choosing a simple random sample of k items from a population of unknown size n in a single pass.

Name: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm
Description: Finds the majority element in an array (the element that appears more than n/2 times) in linear time and constant space.

Name: Topological Sort
Description: A linear ordering of the vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge from vertex u to vertex v, u comes before v.

Name: Cycle Detection in a Linked List
Description: Detects if a linked list has a cycle using Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm.

Name: Flood Fill Algorithm (BFS)
Description: An algorithm that determines the area connected to a given node in a multi-dimensional array.

Name: Trapping Rain Water
Description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

Name: Find the Celebrity
Description: In a party of n people, a celebrity is a person who is known by everyone but knows no one. This finds the celebrity in O(n) time.

Name: sort
Description: Parent category for various sorting algorithms in Java.

Name: Bubble Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

Name: Selection Sort
Description: An in-place comparison sorting algorithm that divides the input list into a sorted and an unsorted sublist.

Name: Insertion Sort
Description: A simple sorting algorithm that builds the final sorted array one item at a time.

Name: Merge Sort
Description: An efficient, stable, comparison-based sorting algorithm based on the divide and conquer paradigm.

Name: Quick Sort
Description: An efficient sorting algorithm that works by selecting a 'pivot' element and partitioning the other elements into two sub-arrays.

Name: Heap Sort
Description: A comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure.

Name: Counting Sort
Description: An integer sorting algorithm that operates by counting the number of objects that have each distinct key value.

Name: Radix Sort
Description: A non-comparative sorting algorithm that sorts integers by processing individual digits.

Name: Bucket Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm that works by distributing the elements of an array into a number of buckets.

Name: Shell Sort
Description: A highly efficient, in-place, comparison-based sorting algorithm which is a generalization of insertion sort.

Name: Comb Sort
Description: Improves on Bubble Sort by using a gap of size more than 1, helping to eliminate 'turtles' quickly.

Name: Pancake Sort
Description: The only allowed operation is to flip a prefix of the array. The goal is to sort the array with the minimum number of flips.

Name: Gnome Sort
Description: A sorting algorithm similar to insertion sort but moving the element to its proper place is accomplished by a series of swaps.

Name: Cocktail Shaker Sort
Description: A variation of Bubble Sort that sorts in both directions on each pass through the list.

Name: Arrays.sort (TimSort)
Description: The built-in sort method in Java. For object arrays, it uses TimSort, a stable, adaptive, iterative mergesort.

Name: data_structure
Description: Parent category for various data structures in Java.

Name: Singly Linked List
Description: A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes, and each node points to the next node in the sequence.

Name: Stack
Description: Represents a last-in, first-out (LIFO) stack of objects.

Name: Queue
Description: A collection designed for holding elements prior to processing. FIFO (first-in-first-out) is a typical way of ordering elements.

Name: Binary Search Tree
Description: A node-based binary tree data structure with the property that the left subtree of a node contains only nodes with keys lesser than the node’s key.

Name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
Description: A tree-like data structure that proves to be quite efficient for solving problems related to strings and prefixes.

Name: Min-Heap (PriorityQueue)
Description: A specialized data structure that satisfies the heap property. Java's PriorityQueue is a Min-Heap implementation.

Name: Hash Table (HashMap)
Description: A data structure that maps keys to values. The HashMap class is the Java implementation of a hash table.

Name: Doubly Linked List (LinkedList)
Description: The built-in LinkedList in Java is a generic doubly linked list, allowing insertion and deletion from both ends.

Name: Graph (Adjacency List)
Description: A data structure representing a graph as an array of adjacency lists.

Name: Disjoint Set Union (DSU)
Description: A data structure that stores a collection of disjoint sets, commonly used for cycle detection in graphs.

Name: HashSet
Description: Implements the Set interface, backed by a hash table. It does not store duplicate elements.

Name: TreeMap
Description: A Red-Black tree based NavigableMap implementation. The map is sorted according to the natural ordering of its keys.

Name: Max-Heap (PriorityQueue)
Description: A Max-Heap can be implemented using PriorityQueue by providing a reverse order comparator.

Name: Deque (ArrayDeque)
Description: A linear collection that supports element insertion and removal at both ends. ArrayDeque is the resizable-array implementation.

Name: ArrayList
Description: Resizable-array implementation of the List interface. Implements all optional list operations, and permits all elements, including null.

Name: search
Description: Parent category for various search algorithms in Java.

Name: Linear Search
Description: A method for finding an element within a list. It sequentially checks each element of the list until a match is found or the whole list has been searched.

Name: Binary Search (Iterative)
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding an item from a sorted list of items by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.

Name: Binary Search (Recursive)
Description: A recursive implementation of the binary search algorithm.

Name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. The algorithm starts at the root node and explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

Name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Description: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. It explores the neighbor nodes at the present depth prior to moving on to nodes at the next depth level.

Name: Arrays.binarySearch
Description: Uses the built-in static method from the Arrays class to perform a binary search on a sorted array.

Name: Stream API findFirst
Description: Returns an Optional describing the first element of this stream, or an empty Optional if the stream is empty.

Name: Jump Search
Description: A searching algorithm for sorted arrays. The basic idea is to check fewer elements by jumping ahead by fixed steps.

Name: Interpolation Search
Description: An improvement over Binary Search for instances where the values in a sorted array are uniformly distributed.

Name: Exponential Search
Description: An algorithm for searching sorted lists. It works by first finding a range where the element might be and then performing a binary search.

Name: Ternary Search
Description: A decrease and conquer search algorithm that splits the array into three parts and determines which part the key lies in.

Name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Finds the minimum element in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Searches for a target value in a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot.

Name: Find First and Last Position of Element
Description: Finds the starting and ending position of a given target value in a sorted array.

Name: Find Peak Element
Description: A peak element is an element that is strictly greater than its neighbors. This finds any peak element in O(log n) time.

Name: math
Description: Parent category for various mathematical algorithms in Java.

Name: Factorial
Description: Calculates the factorial of a non-negative integer using BigInteger to handle large numbers.

Name: Fibonacci Sequence
Description: Generates the Fibonacci sequence up to the nth term.

Name: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
Description: Finds the largest positive integer that divides two integers without leaving a remainder using the Euclidean algorithm.

Name: Check for Prime Number
Description: Checks if a given integer is a prime number.

Name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
Description: An efficient algorithm for finding all prime numbers up to a specified integer.

Name: Modular Exponentiation
Description: Calculates (base^exp) % mod efficiently, also known as power in modular arithmetic.

Name: Combination (nCr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose r items from a set of n items without regard to order.

Name: Permutation (nPr)
Description: Calculates the number of ways to choose and arrange r items from a set of n items.

Name: Palindrome Number Check
Description: Checks if a number is a palindrome without converting it to a string.

Name: Matrix Multiplication
Description: Performs matrix multiplication on two given matrices.

Name: Catalan Numbers
Description: Calculates the nth Catalan number using dynamic programming.

Name: Euler's Totient Function
Description: Counts the positive integers up to a given integer n that are relatively prime to n.

Name: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
Description: Calculates the smallest positive integer that is divisible by both a and b.

Name: Integer Square Root
Description: Calculates the floor of the square root of an integer using binary search to prevent overflow.

Name: Happy Number
Description: A happy number is a number defined by replacing it with the sum of the squares of its digits until it equals 1 or enters a cycle.

Name: dynamic_programming
Description: Parent category for dynamic programming algorithms in Java.

Name: Fibonacci (Memoization)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a top-down dynamic programming approach (memoization).

Name: Fibonacci (Tabulation)
Description: Calculates the nth Fibonacci number using a bottom-up dynamic programming approach (tabulation).

Name: 0/1 Knapsack Problem
Description: Given weights and values of n items, put these items in a knapsack of capacity W to get the maximum total value.

Name: Longest Common Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence present in two given sequences.

Name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
Description: Finds the length of the longest subsequence of a given sequence in which the subsequence's elements are in sorted order.

Name: Edit Distance
Description: Finds the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions or substitutions) required to change one word into the other.

Name: Coin Change Problem
Description: Finds the minimum number of coins that you need to make up a given amount.

Name: Subset Sum Problem
Description: Determines if a given set can be partitioned into two subsets such that the sum of elements in both subsets is the same.

Name: Rod Cutting Problem
Description: Given a rod of length n and prices for pieces, determine the maximum value obtainable by cutting up the rod and selling the pieces.

Name: Word Break Problem
Description: Determine if a string can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.

Name: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's)
Description: Finds the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers which has the largest sum.

Name: Binomial Coefficient
Description: Calculates the binomial coefficient C(n, k) using dynamic programming with space optimization.

Name: Unbounded Knapsack
Description: A variation of the knapsack problem where we can use one or more instances of any item.

Name: House Robber
Description: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. You cannot rob adjacent houses. Find the maximum amount of money you can rob.

Name: Climbing Stairs
Description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

Name: strings
Description: Parent category for string manipulation algorithms in Java.

Name: Check for Palindrome
Description: Checks if a given string is a palindrome, ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.

Name: Reverse a String
Description: Reverses the characters of a given string.

Name: Check for Anagrams
Description: Checks if two strings are anagrams of each other (contain the same characters in different orders).

Name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Description: Finds the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Name: Valid Parentheses
Description: Determines if a string of parentheses is valid (e.g., '()[]{}' is valid).

Name: String Contains Substring (indexOf)
Description: Finds the first occurrence of a substring (needle) in a string (haystack) using the built-in indexOf method.

Name: Group Anagrams
Description: Groups a list of strings into sublists of anagrams.

Name: Remove Duplicate Characters
Description: Removes duplicate characters from a string while preserving original order.

Name: Count Vowels and Consonants
Description: Counts the number of vowels and consonants in a given string.

Name: String to Integer (atoi)
Description: Converts a string to an integer, handling whitespace, signs, and overflow.

Name: Longest Common Prefix
Description: Finds the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings.

Name: Reverse Words in a String
Description: Reverses the order of words in a given string.

Name: Manacher's Algorithm
Description: An algorithm to find the longest palindromic substring in any given string in linear time.

Name: Rabin-Karp Algorithm
Description: A string-searching algorithm that uses hashing to find any one of a set of pattern strings in a text.

Name: Caesar Cipher
Description: A simple substitution cipher where each letter in the plaintext is shifted a certain number of places down or up the alphabet.

Name: backtracking
Description: Parent category for backtracking algorithms in Java.

Name: Generate All Permutations
Description: Generates all possible permutations of a given list of numbers.

Name: Generate All Subsets (Power Set)
Description: Generates all possible subsets (the power set) of a given list of numbers.

Name: N-Queens Problem
Description: The N-Queens puzzle is the problem of placing N chess queens on an N×N chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other.

Name: Sudoku Solver
Description: Solves a Sudoku puzzle using a backtracking algorithm.

Name: Combination Sum
Description: Finds all unique combinations in a list of candidate numbers where the numbers sum to a target.

Name: Word Search
Description: Given a 2D board and a word, find if the word exists in the grid.

Name: Generate Parentheses
Description: Generates all combinations of well-formed parentheses for a given number of pairs.

Name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
Description: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent.

Name: Palindrome Partitioning
Description: Given a string, partition it such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome.

Name: Permutations II (with duplicates)
Description: Given a collection of numbers that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations.

Name: Beautiful Arrangement
Description: Find the number of beautiful arrangements of numbers from 1 to n.

Name: Restore IP Addresses
Description: Given a string containing only digits, restore it by returning all possible valid IP address combinations.

Name: Rat in a Maze
Description: A rat starts from source (0,0) and has to reach the destination (N-1, N-1).

Name: M-Coloring Problem
Description: A problem of coloring the vertices of a graph with at most m colors such that no two adjacent vertices have the same color.

Name: Path with Maximum Gold
Description: In a gold mine grid, find the path that collects the most gold.

Name: bit_manipulation
Description: Parent category for bit manipulation algorithms in Java.

Name: Check if a number is even or odd
Description: Checks if a number is even or odd using the bitwise AND operator.

Name: Count set bits
Description: Counts the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of an integer using Brian Kernighan's algorithm.

Name: Check if the i-th bit is set
Description: Checks if the bit at the i-th position is set to 1.

Name: Set the i-th bit
Description: Sets the bit at the i-th position to 1.

Name: Clear the i-th bit
Description: Clears the bit at the i-th position (sets it to 0).

Name: Toggle the i-th bit
Description: Toggles the bit at the i-th position (flips 0 to 1 and 1 to 0).

Name: Check if a number is a power of 2
Description: Checks if a given positive integer is a power of two.

Name: Find the single number in an array
Description: Given a non-empty array of integers where every element appears twice except for one, find that single one.

Name: Swap two numbers
Description: Swaps the values of two integer variables using XOR operations. (Note: Java is pass-by-value).

Name: Find the two non-repeating elements
Description: Finds the two elements that appear only once in an array where all other elements appear twice.

Name: Gray Code Conversion
Description: Converts a binary number to its Gray code equivalent.

Name: Reverse bits of a given integer
Description: Reverses the bits of a 32-bit signed integer.

Name: Sum of two integers without using + and -
Description: Calculates the sum of two integers using bitwise operations.

Name: Find position of the only set bit
Description: If a number has only one set bit, this function returns the 1-based position of that bit.

Name: Number of Flips to Convert A to B
Description: Counts the number of bits you would need to flip to get from one integer to another by counting set bits in their XOR.

Name: other
Description: Parent category for miscellaneous but important algorithms in Java.

Name: Two Sum Problem
Description: Given an array of integers, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to a specific target.

Name: Kadane's Algorithm
Description: An efficient algorithm to find the contiguous subarray within a one-dimensional array of numbers that has the largest sum.

Name: Sliding Window Maximum
Description: Given an array and an integer k, find the maximum for each and every contiguous subarray of size k.

Name: LRU Cache
Description: A Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache organizes items in order of use. This implementation leverages LinkedHashMap.

Name: Meeting Rooms II
Description: Given an array of meeting time intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

Name: Next Permutation
Description: Implement the next lexicographical permutation of a list of numbers.

Name: Dutch National Flag Problem
Description: Given an array containing 0s, 1s, and 2s (representing colors), sort them in-place.

Name: Josephus Problem
Description: In a circle of n people, every k-th person is eliminated. The problem is to find the position (0-indexed) of the last person remaining.

Name: Reservoir Sampling
Description: A randomized algorithm for choosing a simple random sample of k items from a population of unknown size n in a single pass.

Name: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm
Description: Finds the majority element in an array (the element that appears more than n/2 times) in linear time and constant space.

Name: Topological Sort
Description: A linear ordering of the vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge from vertex u to vertex v, u comes before v.

Name: Cycle Detection in a Linked List
Description: Detects if a linked list has a cycle using Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm.

Name: Flood Fill Algorithm (BFS)
Description: An algorithm that determines the area connected to a given node in a multi-dimensional array.

Name: Trapping Rain Water
Description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

Name: Find the Celebrity
Description: In a party of n people, a celebrity is a person who is known by everyone but knows no one. This finds the celebrity in O(n) time.
